summary: Stancil argues that social media’s attention mechanics push people toward performative extremity, and that attention has shifted from a path to power into the power itself. As a result, the influencer logic now governs politics, business, and culture, creating a leadership class selected for virality rather than competence or decorum.
money_quotes: Attention is no longer a bridge to power; it is the power.
Everyone is forged in the same fever, by the same game that rewards the same behavior: Be unhinged.
Elon Musk is not the richest man in the world despite acting manic; he is richest man in the world because of it.
If it’s easier to turn attention into capital than it is to turn capital into attention, then, over time, the influencers will crowd out the management class.
Social media didn’t start the fire, but is it putting the arsons in charge?
key_insight: The attention economy has fused celebrity with authority, so the people who can go viral increasingly become the people who run everything.
